tasks.title: ADR e contrato: rag-sqlite opcional no Conversation ESAA v1.2
tasks.description: Formalizar o plano Codex de integração opt-in do rag-sqlite: ADR, contrato CLI (rag enable/status/refresh/disable/search), schema conversation-esaa.search.v1, privacidade, .gitignore e limites (Ollama local only, projeção descartável, fail-open no pipeline principal). Fonte: docs/plans/v1.2-optional-rag-sqlite.md
acceptance_criteria: ADR e contrato CLI documentados com schema search.v1 e estados RagNotReady/RagUnavailable/stale
PRIVACY e gitignore excluem .conversation-esaa/rag/ e artefatos reconstruíveis
Escopo v1.2: Ollama loopback only; SQLite é projeção descartável; verify sem rede

tasks.title: Adaptador PowerShell: config, exportador e conversation-esaa search
tasks.description: Implementar adaptador PS e fachada em conversation-esaa.ps1: rag enable|status|refresh|disable|disable --purge; search com JSON conversation-esaa.search.v1; export determinístico por event_id em .conversation-esaa/rag/corpus; reidratação de metadados a partir de activity.jsonl; sem indexar handoff/state/decisions/topics separadamente.

search devolve conversation-esaa.search.v1 com hits dedupe event_id e untrusted
sem geração válida: RagNotReady/RagUnavailable sem fallback textual silencioso

remote Ollama URL rejected; search schema v1 with untrusted hits

tasks.title: Worker assíncrono: dirty marker, lock, debounce e refresh
tasks.description: Após project+verify bem-sucedidos, gravar dirty marker e agendar worker one-shot (debounce 10s, lock próprio). Worker exporta faltantes, reconcilia exclusões, rag-sqlite index --sync --prune, reprocessa se dirty avançou. Falhas preservam índice anterior e dirty; hooks não embutem embeddings nem bloqueiam >1s.
acceptance_criteria: dois syncs concorrentes = um worker efetivo; sem corpus parcial
falha Ollama mantém geração anterior + dirty; pipeline principal saudável
search defasado retorna stale:true com event_lag e indexed_at
agendamento no hook <=1s; embeddings fora do lock principal

checks: worker lock + debounce; refresh preserves dirty on failure
search returns RagNotReady/stale without usable generation

smoke piloto recupera TOP-011, DEC-0035 e CONV-003/TASK-014 no top
disable --purge remove só artefatos reconstruíveis; permissões 0700/0600 no Unix
RELEASE/README documentam opt-in v1.2 e dependências não instaladas automaticamente

checks: RELEASE and README document opt-in v1.2

tasks.title: Propagar falhas e validar o protocolo do rag-sqlite
tasks.description: Corrigir o adaptador em conv-rag.ps1 para preservar exit_code, stdout e stderr do processo externo; interpretar somente stdout JSON válido; aceitar sucesso com exit 0 e, apenas para index vazio, exit 2 acompanhado de rag_sqlite.index.v1, ok=true e totals.files=0. Saída vazia ou inválida deve virar RagProtocolError; exit não permitido ou payload ok=false deve virar RagCommandFailed. Enable só publica configuração após todos os comandos config terem sucesso. Refresh malsucedido preserva geração, activity_lines e dirty marker. CLI explícita retorna exit 1 em falha; search mantém erros tipados e exit 2; worker pode falhar sem bloquear sync/project/hooks. Atualizar contrato e schema de erro sem expor stdout/stderr integralmente.
acceptance_criteria: Nenhuma falha, saída vazia ou JSON inválido do rag-sqlite produz ok=true.
Exit 2 só é aceito como sucesso para index vazio com payload válido e files=0.
Falha de refresh preserva geração anterior, activity_lines e dirty marker; enable parcial não substitui configuração válida.
Enable e refresh explícitos retornam erro tipado e exit 1; search indisponível retorna erro tipado e exit 2.
Falha do RAG continua fail-open para sync, project e hooks.
required_verification: Executar tests/test-rag-command-contract.ps1 com stub temporário para exit 0, 1, 2 e 9.
Cobrir JSON válido, payload ok=false, stdout vazio ou inválido e stderr separado.
Confirmar que exit 2 válido com zero arquivos é sucesso e qualquer outro exit 2 é falha.
Confirmar dirty marker, geração anterior e activity_lines preservados após falha.
Confirmar os exit codes públicos e que o pipeline conversacional permanece fail-open.

checks: tests/test-rag-command-contract.ps1: ALL PASSED (exit 0, stubs exit 0/1/2/9, protocol, dirty/generation preserve, public exits, worker fail-open)
Invoke-RagSqlite uses ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList + separate stdout/stderr; exit2 empty index only; enable atomic config publish

tasks.title: Instalar o adaptador RAG pelo bootstrap
tasks.description: Adicionar conv-rag.ps1 à lista de engines copiados por conv-bootstrap.ps1. Mesclar de forma idempotente as entradas privadas do .gitignore, incluindo .conversation-esaa/rag/, preservando conteúdo, ordem e sentinelas existentes e sem duplicar padrões. O bootstrap não pode habilitar RAG, criar índice ou chamar Ollama. Corrigir test-rag-adapter.ps1 para executar exclusivamente conversation-esaa.ps1 e conv-rag.ps1 copiados para o workspace temporário, nunca os caminhos do repositório-fonte.
acceptance_criteria: Bootstrap limpo instala conv-rag.ps1 com hash igual ao arquivo-fonte.
Search pelo CLI instalado retorna RagUnavailable e exit 2 enquanto o RAG está desabilitado.
Quando as dependências estão disponíveis, enable, refresh e search funcionam pelo CLI instalado.
Rerun do bootstrap preserva event store e conteúdo personalizado do .gitignore e mantém uma única entrada .conversation-esaa/rag/.
Bootstrap não habilita RAG nem acessa Ollama.
required_verification: Executar bootstrap em diretório temporário limpo e validar presença e hash de conv-rag.ps1.
Executar o bootstrap sobre .gitignore com sentinela personalizada e confirmar preservação e idempotência.
Executar search desabilitado pelo CLI instalado e validar RagUnavailable com exit 2.
Executar tests/test-rag-adapter.ps1 usando somente o runtime instalado no workspace temporário.
Confirmar que bootstrap não criou config.json, index.sqlite ou conexão Ollama.

checks: tests/test-rag-adapter.ps1: ALL PASSED — bootstrap installs conv-rag.ps1 hash match, gitignore idempotent+sentinel, search disabled RagUnavailable exit 2, happy path via installed CLI only
bootstrap does not create config.json/index.sqlite; no Ollama; engines include conv-rag.ps1

tasks.title: Aplicar permissões privadas a toda a projeção RAG
tasks.description: Implementar endurecimento recursivo Unix no adaptador: diretórios 0700 e arquivos regulares 0600, sem seguir symlinks. Cobrir config, state, manifest, corpus, dirty marker, worker lock, logs, index.sqlite, index.sqlite-wal e index.sqlite-shm. Aplicar imediatamente após criar lock ou redirecionamentos e no finally das chamadas ao motor, refresh e worker. Validar o exit code de chmod; falha explícita deve produzir PermissionHardeningFailed, preservar dirty e nunca ser registrada como refresh bem-sucedido. No worker, a falha permanece fail-open para o pipeline conversacional. Não alterar o comportamento Windows.
acceptance_criteria: Após enable, refresh ou worker, todos os diretórios RAG Unix têm modo 0700 e todos os arquivos regulares têm modo 0600.
DB, WAL, SHM, locks e logs são abrangidos pelo endurecimento.
Falha de chmod produz PermissionHardeningFailed, mantém dirty e não resulta em ok=true.
Symlinks não são seguidos e o comportamento Windows permanece inalterado.
Falha do endurecimento no worker não bloqueia o pipeline conversacional.
required_verification: Executar tests/test-rag-permissions.ps1 em workspace temporário Unix.
Criar deliberadamente DB, WAL, SHM, lock e logs permissivos, executar refresh e validar todos os modos com stat.
Simular falha de chmod e confirmar PermissionHardeningFailed, dirty preservado e ausência de falso sucesso.
Confirmar que symlinks não são seguidos.
Confirmar que sync e project permanecem saudáveis quando somente o endurecimento RAG falha.

checks: tests/test-rag-permissions.ps1: ALL PASSED — dirs 0700 files 0600, symlink not followed, chmod fail PermissionHardeningFailed preserves dirty, worker fail-open exit 0
Protect-RagProjection recursive no-follow; chmod exit validated; applied after engine/refresh/worker/lock

tasks.title: Regressão integrada dos hotfixes RAG
tasks.description: Validar em conjunto os hotfixes de execução, bootstrap e permissões sem implementar correções adicionais. Executar os testes específicos, happy path real com rag-sqlite, Ollama embeddinggemma e sqlite-vec, bateria legada, verificações de contrato e governança. Testes não podem modificar arquivos versionados nem deixar workers temporários. Finding novo deve ser reportado e a QA deve solicitar mudanças, não corrigir silenciosamente.
acceptance_criteria: Testes de contrato, permissões e adaptador passam integralmente.
conv-test-battery.ps1 -SkipLab -SkipEsaa mantém no mínimo o baseline de 77 passed e 0 failed.
Bootstrap limpo usa exclusivamente o runtime copiado.
Happy path real confirma rag-sqlite ready, embeddinggemma, sqlite-vec e busca funcional.
git diff --check, equivalência AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md e esaa verify passam.
A execução dos testes não cria alterações versionadas nem deixa workers temporários.
required_verification: Executar tests/test-rag-command-contract.ps1.
Executar tests/test-rag-permissions.ps1.
Executar tests/test-rag-adapter.ps1.
Executar .conversation-esaa/bin/conv-test-battery.ps1 -SkipLab -SkipEsaa e exigir pelo menos 77 passed, 0 failed.
Executar rag-sqlite health e uma busca real com Ollama embeddinggemma e sqlite-vec.
Executar git diff --check, diff AGENTS.md .claude/CLAUDE.md e esaa --root . verify.
Comparar git status antes e depois dos testes e confirmar ausência de novas alterações versionadas.

checks: test-rag-adapter.ps1 ALL PASSED exit 0 (installed runtime only)
real happy path: rag enable, health ok provider_ok model embeddinggemma sqlite-vec available, refresh ok generation 1, search hit_count=1 backend=sqlite-vec
git diff --check clean; AGENTS.md == .claude/CLAUDE.md; esaa verify ok last_event_seq=463
git status before/after identical; no leftover rag workers; no .conversation-esaa/rag in product repo

tasks.title: Specify Conversation ESAA installer v1.3
tasks.description: Define the decision-complete architecture and public contract for conversation-esaa@1.3.0. The npm/npx installer is the primary UX, conv-bootstrap.ps1 remains the no-Node fallback, and ESAA-Core is out of scope. Cover interactive and non-interactive installation, four current agents, optional RAG, Codex watcher services, ownership manifests, update/repair/uninstall safety, JSON output, dry-run, privacy, and partial failures.
acceptance_criteria: The contract defines install, status, doctor, update, repair, and uninstall including flags and exit semantics.
The contract preserves activity.jsonl, projections, and RAG data during uninstall and upgrades.
The design is consistent with ADR-010, PRIVACY.md, and AGENTS.md.

checks: ADR and CLI contract cover install, status, doctor, update, repair, and uninstall.
Design cross-checked against ADR-010, PRIVACY.md, and AGENTS.md.

tasks.title: Make conv-bootstrap a selective installation engine
tasks.description: Extend conv-bootstrap.ps1 as the low-level effect engine for the npm installer while preserving its current no-Node fallback. Add selected-agent, JSON, and dry-run inputs. Preserve all private Conversation ESAA state and fail closed on invalid existing agent configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Only selected agents are configured and repeated installation is idempotent.
No activity log, projection, sync state, or RAG data is overwritten.
Legacy invocation without agent-selection arguments remains supported.

checks: PowerShell bootstrap integration test passed for selection, idempotency, private-state preservation, and dry-run.

tasks.title: Build conversation-esaa npm installer core
tasks.description: Create the conversation-esaa@1.3.0 Node CLI under src with npm package metadata. Implement environment detection, interactive prompts, cwd/--workspace targeting, selected-agent input, non-interactive validation, JSON output, dry-run, install state, file ownership, and SHA-256 manifest. Invoke PowerShell through argument arrays without shell interpolation.
acceptance_criteria: npx conversation-esaa install supports interactive, non-interactive, --json, and --dry-run paths.
The package records owned versus merged files and never captures private conversation data.
npm pack installs and runs from a clean temporary workspace.

checks: npm pack installed and executed from a clean temporary workspace; Conversation ESAA verify returned ok.

tasks.title: Implement Grok Claude Codex and Antigravity installer adapters
tasks.description: Implement adapter detection and safe configuration for Grok, Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity. Grok trust changes and Codex persistent services require confirmation. Claude and Antigravity configs are structurally merged. Codex uses the real absolute pwsh path and supports manual watcher, Linux systemd user, or Windows Scheduled Task.
acceptance_criteria: Existing unrelated hooks and configuration survive install, repair, update, and uninstall.
Invalid JSON fails closed without overwriting files.
Persistent watcher activation is never performed without explicit confirmation.

checks: Agent adapter suite and existing installer tests passed: 7 tests.
Invalid JSON fail-closed and unrelated Claude/Antigravity settings remained intact.

tasks.title: Add assisted existing and managed rag-sqlite setup
tasks.description: Implement RAG modes off, existing, and managed. External fail-closed prerequisite: public elzobrito/rag-sqlite v0.1.0 release with ZIP and SHA256SUMS. Existing mode validates the resolved command with schema query. Managed mode downloads only the pinned release over HTTPS, verifies SHA-256, extracts safely into the per-user data directory, and records version/checksum. Ollama is never installed silently; pulling embeddinggemma requires confirmation. RAG enables only after schema and health succeed.
acceptance_criteria: Checksum mismatch, unsafe archive entry, missing Python, and invalid schema are rejected.
Optional RAG failure leaves the core installation intact, RAG disabled, and doctor guidance available.
Existing rag-sqlite and Ollama installations are reused without replacement.

checks: Combined installer, adapter, and RAG tests passed: 11 tests.
Pinned release checksum matches the independently published rag-sqlite v0.1.0 asset.

tasks.title: Implement installer status doctor update repair and uninstall
tasks.description: Implement lifecycle commands using the install state and ownership manifest. Classify managed files as intact, modified, or missing. Update must preserve user modifications. Repair must not overwrite modified files without confirmation. Uninstall removes only managed runtime, hooks, and services, and always preserves Conversation ESAA history, projections, sync state, and RAG data.
acceptance_criteria: doctor validates PowerShell, hooks, watcher, install manifest, conversation-esaa verify, and optional RAG.
Update and repair are idempotent and preserve customized files.
Uninstall preserves all private and canonical conversation data.

checks: Complete Node installer suite passed: 15 tests.
Lifecycle tests verify modified-file preservation and private activity survival after uninstall.

tasks.title: Validate Conversation ESAA installer end to end
tasks.description: Independently validate the npm tarball on Ubuntu and Windows with Node 20/22 and PowerShell 7. Cover each agent alone, all agents, existing hooks, paths with spaces and Unicode, reinstall, dry-run, JSON, update, repair, uninstall with history, RAG existing/managed, Codex service generation, archive safety, and command injection resistance.
acceptance_criteria: The tarball passes clean install and doctor on Ubuntu and Windows.
Existing Conversation ESAA test batteries and RAG contract tests remain green.
diff AGENTS.md .claude/CLAUDE.md is empty and esaa verify is ok.

checks: npm installer suite passed: 19 tests including live managed RAG release consumption.
Legacy Conversation ESAA suite passed: 77 passed, 0 failed.
RAG adapter, command-contract, and permissions suites all passed.
AGENTS.md and .claude/CLAUDE.md are identical; ESAA verify is ok.

tasks.title: Prepare Conversation ESAA installer v1.3.0 release candidate
tasks.description: Prepare the reviewed npm release candidate. Update README, RELEASE, AGENTS.md, and .claude/CLAUDE.md with the npx-first workflow, PowerShell fallback, supported agents, RAG modes, lifecycle commands, privacy, and operational limits. Preserve byte-identical AGENTS.md and .claude/CLAUDE.md. Validate npm pack contents and install only from the generated tgz.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation begins with the npx install path and retains the PowerShell fallback.
The npm tarball contains no private logs, projections, databases, or local credentials.
A clean workspace installed only from tgz passes doctor.

tasks.title: Publish conversation-esaa 1.3.0 to npm and GitHub
tasks.description: Using human-terminal with authenticated npm and gh, publish conversation-esaa@1.3.0 to npm and the matching GitHub Release from the reviewed commit. Do not modify source files. Verify the public registry and npx path after publication.
acceptance_criteria: npm view conversation-esaa@1.3.0 confirms the reviewed version.
npx conversation-esaa@1.3.0 doctor runs from the public registry.
The GitHub release points to the reviewed commit and documents artifact checksums.
